name: Julianna Peña

qualityWinLedger.context: Submitted the dominant reigning UFC two-division champion to win the bantamweight title.
Strong contender win over a former UFC title challenger.
Won the UFC bantamweight title by disputed split decision; title value is respected but capped below full top-five credit.
Ranked bantamweight/flyweight contender and useful early UFC win.
Olympic medalist and ranked bantamweight veteran in Peña’s title climb.
Former UFC flyweight champion name, capped for inactivity, division movement, and limited sustained elite proof.
Supporting UFC finish with limited opponent-quality value.
TUF finale support win with minimal long-term opponent-quality value.

primeWindowDetail.endReason: Tate win and Nunes upset begin title-level window. Nunes II does not close it because Peña remains title-relevant/current.

lossContextEvents.context: Peña remained title-relevant and won the Pennington title fight.
Canonical current window remains open unless a later endpoint is approved.

shortCase: Peña is the giant-upset title case: she beat Amanda Nunes and later won a second UFC bantamweight title.
bestArgument: She owns one of the biggest UFC title wins ever.
counterArgument: The counter is dominance: no defenses, decisive rematch loss, and Kayla loss.
whyRankedHere: Peña scores this high because beating Amanda Nunes for the belt is a gigantic UFC-only title result, and the Pennington title win gives her a second championship point.
whyNotHigher: She does not rank higher because she has zero defenses, the Nunes rematch was decisive, and the Kayla/Valentina/GDR losses keep the dominance case low.
finalTakeaway: Peña is weird but necessary: a huge UFC championship résumé without a clean dominance résumé.
